description: Test independence of two categorical variables using a chi-square contingency table — comparing observed joint frequencies against the frequencies expected under the null of independence

fields.name: contingency_table
fields.label: r x c matrix of observed joint frequencies O_ij

fields.name: row_labels
fields.label: Labels for rows (r categories of variable 1)

fields.name: column_labels
fields.label: Labels for columns (c categories of variable 2)

core_formulas.distribution: Chi-square with (r-1)(c-1) degrees of freedom under H0 of independence

hypotheses: H0: the two categorical variables are independent
hypotheses.alternative: Ha: the variables are NOT independent (i.e., associated)

expected_cell_size_rule.rule_of_thumb: Every expected cell count >= 5 for chi-square approximation to hold
expected_cell_size_rule.fix_when_violated: Combine sparse categories or use Fisher's exact test

interpretation.large_chi_square: Observed and expected frequencies diverge; reject independence
interpretation.small_chi_square: Observed closely matches expected; fail to reject independence
interpretation.effect_size: Cramer's V = sqrt(chi_square / (n * min(r-1, c-1))) — 0 to 1, interprets as correlation

investment_applications.sector_and_return_class: Test whether sector membership is independent of the return bucket (winner/loser/flat)
investment_applications.rating_migrations: Test independence between prior rating and subsequent rating change
investment_applications.factor_exposures: Test independence between style (value/growth) and risk bucket
investment_applications.survey_analysis: Test independence of wealth segment and risk tolerance

run_chi_square_test.description: Compute chi-square statistic and compare to critical value

low_expected_frequencies.description: At least one expected cell is too sparse for chi-square approximation

table_malformed.description: Contingency table is not a valid r x c matrix

errors.message: Contingency table must be a rectangular r x c matrix with non-negative counts

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply the chi-square test of independence when expected cell counts are below 5
anti_patterns.why: The chi-square distribution is an approximation; sparse cells violate the approximation and produce unreliable p-values. Use Fisher's exact test instead

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret a significant chi-square as indicating the DIRECTION of the association
anti_patterns.why: The test only rejects independence; it tells you NOTHING about which categories drive the dependence. Inspect residuals (O-E)/sqrt(E) for direction

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use chi-square independence for paired categorical data
anti_patterns.why: McNemar's test is the correct choice for paired categorical data (e.g., rating migrations for the same firm over time)

anti_patterns.rule: Do not conflate independence with homogeneity
anti_patterns.why: Chi-square independence tests joint distribution; chi-square homogeneity tests equal distributions across groups. Formulas match, interpretation differs
